title: verific: New `-sva-continue-on-error` import option
body:
This option allows you to process a design that includes unsupported SVA. Unsupported SVA gets imported as formal cells using 'x inputs and with the `unsupported_sva` attribute set. 

_What are the reasons/motivation for this change?_

This enables limited processing of designs with unsupported SVA, e.g. to inspect the design hierarchy including all properties, or to explicitly select a subset of supported properties to perform FV with. To ensure that this can be safely used without the risk of silently ignored properties, `hierarchy -smtcheck` (used by SBY) will by error out if the user script leaves any unsupported properties around, making sure both parsing unsupported SVA and performing FV while ignoring unsupported SVA require separate opt-ins.

_Explain how this is achieved._

This mostly reuses existing internal debug functionality of verificsva to recover from unsupported SVA but adds additional logging error handling, emission of `unsupported_sva`-marked placeholder property cells and testing.

_If applicable, please suggest to reviewers how they can test the change._

The added tests cover all new functionality. Since the added safeguards extend existing checks used by SBY that we can fully test within yosys, this requires no separate SBY changes or tests.
